title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 3
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: An international student organisation is looking at the impact of technology on students' social lives. You have been asked to write a report for this organisation, describing the current situation in your country and recommending ways to prevent social isolation.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Technology and Social Cohesion among Students: A National Perspective

Introduction
The aim of this report is to analyse the impact of digital technology on the social lives of students in my country and to propose strategies to mitigate the risk of social isolation.

The Current Situation
In recent years, the nature of student socialisation has shifted dramatically towards digital platforms. While social media allows for constant connectivity, there is evidence that the quality of interactions is declining. A recent survey indicated that 60% of students spend more than four hours daily on their devices, often at the expense of face-to-face contact. Furthermore, the rise of online learning has reduced the opportunities for spontaneous peer interaction, leading many to report feelings of loneliness despite being 'connected' online.

The Risk of Isolation
The primary concern is that digital interaction lacks the emotional depth of physical presence. This can lead to a 'echo chamber' effect, where students only interact with those who share their views, hindering personal growth. Additionally, the pressure to maintain a perfect online persona can contribute to social anxiety, making students more hesitant to engage in real-world social settings.

Recommendations
To combat this trend, I recommend the following:
- Tech-Free Zones: Universities should designate specific areas in student unions where phone use is discouraged to promote conversation.
- Mentorship Programmes: Pairing senior students with newcomers for regular in-person meetups can provide a necessary social anchor.
- Hybrid Events: Clubs should prioritise 'physical-first' events, using technology only as a tool for organisation rather than the venue itself.

Conclusion
Technology is an undeniable part of student life, but it must not replace human connection. By fostering physical spaces for interaction, we can ensure that students remain socially integrated.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt: You have read an article in a newspaper which argues that young people today are lazy and have no interest in local politics. Write a letter to the editor of the newspaper, expressing your disagreement and giving examples of how young people are involved in your community.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Editor,

I am writing to express my strong disagreement with the views expressed in last Tuesday's article regarding the supposed 'laziness' and political apathy of today’s youth. As a young person deeply involved in my local community, I found the author's generalisations not only inaccurate but also dismissive of the hard work many of us perform daily.

Contrary to the article's claims, young people in our city are more politically and socially active than ever before. For instance, the 'Green Future' initiative, which successfully lobbied the local council to increase cycle lanes, was entirely founded and managed by university students. This demonstrates that far from being uninterested, we are simply choosing to engage with politics through direct, issue-based activism rather than traditional party lines.

Furthermore, the local youth centre survives largely due to a dedicated team of teenage volunteers who provide tutoring and sports coaching to younger children. These individuals spend their weekends giving back to the community, which hardly fits the description of being 'lazy'. 

The reality is that the digital age has provided us with new tools to organise and effect change. We are deeply concerned about the future, particularly regarding climate change and economic inequality, and we are working tirelessly to address these issues at a local level.

I hope that in the future, your newspaper will seek to highlight these positive contributions rather than relying on tired stereotypes. We are not apathetic; we are simply active in ways that the author has failed to recognise.

Yours faithfully,
Liam O'Connor

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have been asked to write a review for a careers website about a website or app that helps people find jobs or prepare for interviews. Explain how it works, what its best features are, and whether you would recommend it to others.
tasks.modelAnswer:
CareerPath: Your Digital Coach for the Job Market

Navigating the modern job market can be an overwhelming experience, but the 'CareerPath' app aims to simplify the process. Having used the app during my own recent job hunt, I believe it is a game-changer for anyone seeking professional advancement.

The app functions as a comprehensive career assistant. Upon signing up, users upload their CV, which the app’s AI then scans to suggest suitable roles from a variety of global job boards. However, CareerPath goes beyond mere job listings; it also provides tailored advice on how to improve your resume for specific industries.

The standout feature is undoubtedly the 'Mock Interview' module. Using voice recognition and your phone's camera, the app conducts a simulated interview, asking common industry questions and then providing a detailed report on your performance. It analyses everything from your body language to the pace of your speech and the relevance of your answers. For someone like me, who suffers from pre-interview nerves, this was an invaluable tool for building confidence.

Another excellent feature is the 'Salary Tracker', which provides real-time data on average pay for specific roles in different cities, helping users to negotiate more effectively. 

I would wholeheartedly recommend CareerPath to both recent graduates and seasoned professionals. While the premium subscription is slightly pricey, the quality of the interview feedback alone makes it a worthwhile investment. In a competitive world, having an AI coach in your pocket might just give you the edge you need to land your dream role.
